since_last: 37% of 87 AI names green; Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads +2.47%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: The tape: broad selling, 37% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX -0.02% · BTC -2.57% · Gold +1.43% · Nasdaq100 +0.73%. 37% of 87 AI names are green — broad selling. Chip Equipment & AI Hardware is doing the heavy lifting at +2.47%.

cards.headline: Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads, AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is Chip Equipment & AI Hardware at +2.47%; weakest is AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech at -3.17%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: MU is the standout at +16.99%
cards.body: Leaders: MU +16.99%, AMAT +10.85%, TER +8.63%, QCOM +8.41%, KLAC +7.41%. Laggards: INOD -10.96%, DELL -5.97%, PLTR -5.78%, AAPL -5.18%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -1.77% at $151.8
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -1.77% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
